chapter_title: Communicating behavioural expectations to students (Acceptable Use Agreements)
snippet: Template Acceptable Use Agreements are available on the Resources tab and are structured in 2 parts as follows: Schools must review their AUAs every 2 years, as required under Child Safe Standard 10 External Link . Schools must communicate student behavioural expectations regarding the safe, intentional and responsible use of digital technologies and are strongly encouraged to do this by placing a copy of their AUA on their school website or another platform accessible to the school community. They can also determine if and how it should be signed and returned to the school. For example: Schools can also select the most appropriate timeframe for sending AUAs out. For example: Acceptable Use Agreements ensure schools meet the Minimum Standards, including the Child Safe Standards, and other requirements for school registration by: For more information, visit:
